stories.title: Despite Trump's frustration, Spain's Pedro Sánchez doubles down on opposing the war
stories.description: President Trump has been voicing his frustration with the Spanish government's opposition to the war in Iran. But far from backing down, Spain's prime minister is happy to walk into the spotlight.

stories.title: Khameni's killing raises old questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
stories.description: The killing of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei raises questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be in the business of assassinating foreign leaders.

stories.title: Fear of Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz could further slow the flow of oil
stories.description: Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.

stories.title: U.S. investigates strike on Iranian school as the war sparks a global oil crisis
stories.description: Trump, who promised to lower gas prices, is tapping the Strategic Petroleum Reserve as war drives prices up. And, the U.S. investigates the strike on an Iranian school that killed at least 165 people.

stories.title: Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz as U.S.-Israel strikes continue
stories.description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Israel continue airstrikes in the country, triggering what's being described as the "largest oil disruption in history."

stories.title: Iran says its new leader made his 1st address, vowing to keep Strait of Hormuz closed
stories.description: Iran's state media issued what it said was a statement by Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, vowing to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and keep up attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: U.S.-Israel strikes continue as Iran effectively closes Strait of Hormuz, Trump ran on lower gas prices, but the Iran war challenges that, probe points to U.S. being at fault for Iran school strike.

stories.title: The war in Iran, its effect on the Middle East and the growing global oil crisis
stories.description: The latest on the Iran war and the growing oil crisis across the Middle East from Dubai, Iraq, Lebanon and Israel.

stories.title: Threats to the Strait of Hormuz raises concerns about global oil prices
stories.description: The threat of Iran laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz -- the narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil supply travels -- is raising fresh fears about global oil prices.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
stories.description: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

stories.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

stories.title: How Iran war laid bare the world's reliance on Gulf oil and gas
stories.description: Countries around the world are feeling the impact of the conflict and the resulting energy price shock.

stories.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
stories.description: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut suburbs and south Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket barrage
stories.description: Some Israeli strikes also hit other parts of the Lebanese capital, with 12 people reportedly killed along the seafront.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: British man charged in Dubai for alleged filming of Iranian missiles
stories.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

stories.title: Iran steps up attacks on energy targets as tankers hit
stories.description: Iranian attacks are reported in Bahrain and Oman as two foreign fuel tankers are hit near Iraq.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: 'Not appropriate' for Iran to be at World Cup - Trump
stories.description: US president Donald Trump says Iran are "welcome" at the 2026 World Cup but it is not "appropriate" for them to be there.

stories.title: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on Iranian school
stories.description: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

stories.title: Explosions reported on two foreign vessels in the Gulf, authorities say
stories.description: Pressure has intensified on the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important shipping lanes, since Israel and the US attacked Iran last month.

stories.title: EU and UK demand Israel stop surge in West Bank settler violence since Iran war
stories.description: Six Palestinians have been killed during attacks by settlers in the West Bank since the start of Israel's war, the UN says.

stories.title: Why air strikes on Tehran oil facilities are causing black rain
stories.description: Air strikes have damaged at least four oil facilities since US-Israeli attacks on Iran began last month.

stories.title: 'I just want to be able to sleep': Attacks in Iran rock cities and cut power
stories.description: Iranians in Tehran and Karaj tell the BBC they are exhausted and struggling to sleep after 10 days of Israeli and US attacks.

stories.title: What role has cyber warfare played in Iran?
stories.description: Militaries are often cagey about their cyber activities. But the US has hinted at the role it has played.

stories.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
stories.description: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

stories.title: 'Even under missiles we carry on living' - how young Iranians are coping with war
stories.description: Iranians say they are sheltering at home and rarely venturing out on near-empty streets as the US-Israeli bombing campaign continues.

stories.title: Inside the Australian hotel where Iranian footballers escaped to claim asylum
stories.description: Activists played a key role in making contact with the women as they tried to dodge their minders.

stories.title: War expands to central Beirut as Israeli strike kills Iranians in luxury hotel
stories.description: Israel's military says it targeted Quds Force operatives after a return to war with Lebanon's Hezbollah movement, but Iran says they were diplomats.

stories.title: Iran's new leader has never been tested. He now faces an existential battle
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, enters power as the Islamic republic faces its most serious crisis.

stories.title: In maps: Thirteen days of attacks in Iran and the Middle East
stories.description: Three more civilian ships were attacked in the Gulf overnight, killing at least one person.

stories.title: Iranians deeply divided over Mojtaba Khamenei's rise to power
stories.description: While pro-establishment crowds celebrate Khamenei's appointment as his father's successor, others believe it signals no change to how Iran is ruled.

stories.title: 'Night turned into day': Iranians tell of strikes on oil depots
stories.description: Fuel depots were hit in Tehran and Karaj overnight, with videos showing huge fires and plumes of smoke in multiple locations.

stories.title: Who is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader?
stories.description: Many expect the 56-year-old, who has largely kept a low profile, to continue his father's hardline policies.

stories.title: Why did Iran's president apologise?
stories.description: The apology may hint at a political repositioning inside Iran, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azmi.

stories.title: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians reflect on seven days of conflict and where they see their country going next.

stories.title: The final voyage of the Iranian warship sunk by the US
stories.description: The sinking of an Iranian ship by the US near Sri Lanka brings the conflict uncomfortably close to India.

stories.title: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
stories.description: The BBC sees troops and tanks massing near the border, as speculation mounts that Israel is planning a full-scale ground invasion to fight Hezbollah.

stories.title: Trump wants to pick Iran's new leader - will a hostile regime under fire agree?
stories.description: The president's vision of Iran's future could meet fierce opposition, writes chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: 'We couldn't sleep because of fear': Residents flee as Israel pounds south Beirut
stories.description: Communities across huge swathes of Lebanon have been told by Israel to leave their homes due to military action against Hezbollah.

stories.title: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
stories.description: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azimi.

stories.title: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
stories.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

stories.title: 'All red lines have been crossed': Gulf states weigh response to Iranian strikes
stories.description: Retaliatory Iranian strikes on Gulf states have tarnished their image as safe and prosperous, writes the BBC's Barbara Plett-Usher.

stories.title: 'Kill the regime but not people': BBC speaks to Iranians crossing into Turkey
stories.description: Dan Johnson hears from Iranians at a crossing in north-west Iran about their thoughts on the war and regime.

stories.title: Video released by US shows strikes on Iranian vessels near Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Video released by US shows strikes on Iranian vessels near Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz timelapse shows fall in shipping traffic
stories.description: The footage demonstrates the flow of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz in the Gulf since the US-Israeli military offensive began against Iran on 27 February.

stories.title: BBC visits key Dubai port in Iran's firing line
stories.description: Trade at the Middle East's biggest port has been hit hard since Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz began.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war will be over 'very soon', but not this week
stories.description: At his press briefing, the US president claimed the operation was a "tremendous success", but added that he wants to ensure Iran cannot develop nuclear weaponry.

stories.title: Watch: Why the Iran war is pushing up oil prices
stories.description: As turmoil continues in the Middle East, the price of crude oil rose to more than $100 a barrel on Monday.

stories.title: Kuwaiti government high-rise hit by Iranian drone
stories.description: Footage shows flames tearing through the building in the early hours of Sunday, following more attacks on the gulf state.

stories.title: Watch: Huge flames in Tehran after air strikes on oil depots
stories.description: Social media footage shows flames leaping into the air and plumes of smoke above the city's skyline.

stories.title: US allows sale of Russian oil at sea as Middle East war sends energy prices soaring
stories.description: The United States will temporarily allow the sale of Russian oil already at sea, the Treasury Department said on Thursday, as energy prices surged after US-Israeli strikes on Iran triggered war in the Middle East. The license permits cargoes loaded by March 12 to be delivered and...

stories.title: Netanyahu says new Iran leader 'can't show his face in public'
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says that Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei "can't show his face in public" after Tehran issued a first statement in his name that was read out by a TV presenter. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

stories.title: Iran to allow India-flagged tankers to pass through Strait of Hormuz (reports)
stories.description: Iran will reportedly allow Indian-flagged tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz, a conduit for 40% of the South Asian nation's crude imports, an Indian government source told Reuters on Thursday, but an Iranian source outside the country denied any such ​deal was reached. FRANC...

stories.title: Iran trolls Trump with AI-generated Lego propaganda video
US Democrats call for 'swift investigation' into strike on Iranian school
stories.description: A group of 46 mostly Democratic senators on Wednesday sent a letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth seeking answers on the bombing of a school in Iran. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports.

stories.title: Oil prices top $100 as market braces for long conflict
stories.description: Brent crude oil prices have once again topped $100 per barrel, as energy markets brace for the Iran conflict to drag on. US President Donald Trump says he's less concerned about high oil prices than about stopping Iran, while his energy secretary has confirmed the military cannot...

stories.title: US tech giants on Iran's list of potential targets in Gulf countries
stories.description: Iran's Tasnim news agency has published a list of potential targets for Iran on Telegram, including the offices of tech giants such as Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Nvidia in Gulf countries and Israel. Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha for FRANCE 24.

stories.title: Who gives the kill order? AI and the war in Iran
stories.description: Warfare's always evolving, sometimes slowly: Napoleon’s armies traveled only as those of Julius Caesar. Sometimes, faster than commanders can process: see the futile carnage of World War I. The pace of change on the battlefield today? Fast, very fast.

stories.title: Israel strikes central Beirut after threatening to expand Lebanon operations
stories.description: Israel renewed its strikes on Beirut on Thursday, as it threatened to expand operations and seize territory in Lebanon if Hezbollah did not stop its attacks. The Israeli military issued a call to evacuate ahead of the Beirut attack, after having also widened its evacuation warnin...

stories.title: Iran's supreme leader issues first statement since his appointment
stories.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has issued his first statement since his appointment as the war with the United States and Israel rages. The statement was read on state television Thursday by a news anchor. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas He...

stories.title: Middle East war: Dubai targeted by fresh Iranian drone strikes
stories.description: #Dubai was among the targets of fresh Iranian drone strikes early on March 12, alongside Kuwait's airport. This comes after #Iran denounced a #UN Security Council resolution that demanded strikes on the #Gulf countries stop. Eleven civilians have died since the start of Iranian a...

stories.description: As Iran’s new supreme leader calls for the Strait of Hormuz to remain closed, France’s Minister of Armed Forces and Veteran Affairs, Catherine Vautrin, says Paris has no plans to send naval vessels to the strategic waterway. stories.title: Trump says Iran team would risk their 'life and safety' by playing in World Cup
stories.description: Iran's participation in the World Cup seems to be slipping further away on Thursday, with US President Donald Trump saying that Team Melli players would not be “safe” if they came to the United States.

stories.title: Iranian missile strikes residential area in northern Israel
stories.description: Video from northern Israel captured the moment an Iranian missile impacted in a residential area early Friday morning.

stories.title: Thousands rally in Athens protesting US-Israeli attacks on Iran
stories.description: Protesters in Athens have marched to the US Embassy to condemn the US-Israeli attacks on Iran.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israeli strikes killed Iranian nuclear scientists
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said several Iranian nuclear scientists were killed in Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump says war going ‘well’ as Gulf under wave of attacks
stories.description: Israeli strikes kill dozens more in Lebanon, including 5 children among nine people killed in attack on village of Arki.

stories.title: Iran envoy says Tehran will keep Strait of Hormuz open
stories.description: Iran’s UN envoy Amir Saeid Iravani said Tehran will not close the Strait of Hormuz and remains committed to freedom.

stories.title: Qatar rejects attempts to ‘drive wedge’ with US over gas pause
stories.description: Qatari official denies political motives for decision to halt LNG production following Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Air attacks target bases of Iran-backed forces in Iraq
stories.description: Air attacks have targeted military bases across Iraq belonging to Iran-backed forces.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel ‘stronger than ever’ in first speech since Iran war
stories.description: Prime minister says US-Israeli war prevented Iran from moving its nuclear and ballistic projects underground.

stories.title: Fuel shortages bring queues, protests around the world
stories.description: Rising fuel prices have caused long queues and protests around the world as the US-Israeli war on Iran rages.

stories.title: How will the war on Iran impact the US economy?
stories.description: Outcomes depend on how quickly oil tankers can return to the Strait of Hormuz, experts said.

stories.title: Does President Trump have an exit strategy for the war with Iran?
stories.description: United States officials are issuing contradictory statements on the war's timeline and objectives.

stories.title: Iran vows to make Trump pay for ‘grave miscalculation’ if US escalates war
stories.description: Ali Larijani responds to US threat to hit Iran's power infrastructure, saying the 'whole region will go dark'.

stories.title: How oil is at the center of the US-Israel war with Iran
stories.description: As oil prices rise, the US, Israel and Iran seem ready to keep fighting no matter the costs.

stories.title: Israel launches air attack on Beirut during live Al Jazeera broadcast
stories.description: An Israeli air attack struck a building during a live Al Jazeera broadcast in a new "wave of attacks" on Hezbollah.

stories.title: Gunman dead after attack on Michigan synagogue
stories.description: An unidentified shooter was killed on Thursday after exchanging gunfire with security in an attack on a synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, police said. Police said the gunman was dead following the attack on the Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield. Law enforc...

stories.description: An American KC-135 aerial refuelling aircraft crashed in western Iraq while a second plane involved in the incident landed safely, the US military said on Thursday. stories.description: Iran will ​avenge the blood of its martyrs, keep the Strait of Hormuz closed and attack US bases, new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba ‌Khamenei said on Thursday in a statement read out on state television, his first remarks since succeeding his father following his assassination...

stories.description: Accurate GPS is a mainstay of modern life, but because of the Iran war, people in the UAE are finding that their phones think they are out at sea or in cities hundreds of kilometres away. Jamming and spoofing, used as a defensive tactic against missiles and drones, are to blame, ...

stories.description: The United States was responsible for a Tomahawk missile strike on an Iranian elementary school because of a targeting mistake, The New York Times reported on Wednesday. The newspaper, citing US officials, said the investigation into the February 28 attack was ongoing but prelimi...

stories.title: Three brothers of Iraqi origin arrested over US embassy blast in Oslo
stories.description: Norwegian police said on Wednesday that three brothers had been arrested on suspicion of a “terrorist bombing” over a weekend explosion at the US embassy in Oslo, which caused minor damage but no injuries. stories.title: Indian national killed in attack by Iran against U.S.-owned oil tanker Safesea Vishnu in Iraqi waters

stories.description: “We have no additional information on the timing, method, target, or perpetrators of this alleged attack,” the FBI alert in February reportedly said

stories.title: UN Security Council demands Iran halt attacks on Gulf nations
stories.description: The resolution was passed by 13 votes with two abstentions; Iran slams ‘misuse’

stories.title: ‘We won, but don’t want to leave early,’ Trump says; U.S. have knocked out 58 Iranian mine-laying ships so far
stories.description: He seemed to ​signal that the U.S. would continue ⁠the fight for now; U.S. military gave a figure of 16 mine-laying boats destroyed on Tuesday (March 10, 2026)

stories.title: Iran police chief says anti-government protesters treated as 'enemies'
stories.description: The warning comes after the government cracked down on anti-government protests in January, sparked a month before over economic grievances in the sanctions-hit country.

stories.title: U.S. has ‘practically nothing left to target’ in Iran, says Trump: report
stories.description: Trump has given repeated mixed messages about the timing and aims of the war but on Monday (March 9, 2026) he also said the ending could come “soon.”

stories.title: European Council to discuss Iran, Ukraine on March 19 — Costa
stories.description: The situation in Lebanon, the Gaza Strip and the West Bank is also on the agenda

stories.title: Iraqi tankers can cross Strait of Hormuz — Iran’s ambassador to Baghdad
stories.description: Two tankers were attacked in the Iraqi waters a day before the statement

stories.title: US distorts facts claiming that Tehran enriched uranium to 60% — envoy to UN
stories.description: Amir-Saeid Iravani says Washington "ignores the real root of the situation"

stories.title: Iranian General Staff says US refueling aircraft was downed over western Iraq
stories.description: The Central Command of the US armed forces said that the plane had been lost not due to hostile fire or friendly fire

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on Russian oil loaded on vessels before March 12 — Treasury Department
stories.description: The license allows transactions with Russian fuel before April 11

stories.title: Central Iranian city of Arak bombed — TV
stories.description: No information on damage or casualties is available so far

stories.title: Iran warns against attacks on its energy infrastructure
stories.description: A representative of Khatam al-Anbiya, the central headquarters of the Iranian army, says that otherwise Iran would destroy all oil and gas infrastructure of interest to the US in the region

stories.title: During talks, Iran proposed formally sealing its refusal from nuclear ambitions — minister
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi says Washington turned down the proposal

stories.title: Israel attacks Hezbollah command centers in Beirut, southern Lebanon
stories.description: According to the Israeli military, the facilities were used "to advance terror attacks against the State of Israel and its civilians"

stories.title: Iranian diplomat blames US for navigation problems in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iranian Permanent Representative to the United Nations Amir-Saeid Iravani says that Iran is fully committed to the law of the sea

stories.title: Vessels transiting Strait of Hormuz must adhere to wartime laws — Iranian armed forces
stories.description: The Iranian Armed Forces headquarters noted that this will allow the ships to avoid accidental shelling

stories.title: Hormuz and US-Israel compensation: statement by new supreme leader of Iran
stories.description: Mojtaba Khamenei confirmed the deaths of his sister and wife

stories.title: Israel comes under new missile fire from Iran during Netanyahu’s press conference
stories.description: Air raid sirens sounded in the Tel Aviv area

stories.title: US may form international coalition for vessels in Strait of Hormuz — Bessent
stories.description: The US treasury secretary refuted information that Iran had allegedly mined the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Trucks with Russian medicines for Iran cross Azerbaijani-Iranian border — agency
stories.description: According to it, after appropriate procedures the passage of vehicles with medicines sent through the Astara border checkpoint was ensured

stories.title: Conflict in Iran to become 'beginning of end' for United Nations — Iranian MFA
stories.description: Esmaeil Baghaei said this organization failed to take the correct position regarding the aggression against Iran

stories.title: Iranian embassy in Vienna has no information about strikes on 'Taleghan' center
stories.description: According to the Israeli army's press service, the "Taleghan" compound was "utilized by the regime to advance critical capabilities for developing nuclear weapons"

stories.title: Iranian football team still welcome in US for 2026 FIFA World Cup, says Trump
stories.description: The US president still added that he does not believe "it is appropriate that they be there, for their own life and safety"

stories.title: Tehran to leave neighbors alone if US banned from using their territory for strikes — MFA
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei stressed that Iran has no hostile intentions towards the peoples and countries of the region

stories.title: Israel destroys one of Hezbollah financial network offices in Beirut
stories.description: According to the source, there is no information about the dead or injured

stories.title: Vessels may pass through Strait of Hormuz with permission from Iranian Navy — MFA
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said that Iran does not want the strait to become unsafe

stories.title: Arms control is collapsing, as evidenced by strike on Iran — expert
stories.description: Nelson Wong said the global order no longer reflects the complex reality of the modern world

stories.title: Russia, China oppose holding UNSC meeting to discuss Sanctions Committee report on Iran
stories.description: During the procedural vote, 11 Security Council members voted in favor

stories.title: No refugee flow from Middle East yet — Russian Foreign Ministry
stories.description: According to the statement, the environmental damage from the strikes on the oil storages in Tehran and its suburbs has yet to be assessed

stories.title: Iran threatens Israel with imminent destruction
stories.description: The destruction of the Zionist regime and the establishment of an independent Palestinian state on the territory of Palestine, in which all religions and denominations will be represented, is near, the Iranian army said

stories.title: Russia says formulates proposals on Iranian settlement, contacts underway
stories.description: Russian Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova refrained from elaborating on it

stories.title: US makes money on high oil prices but Iran issue is more important — Trump
stories.description: Donald Trump said the United States is the largest oil producer in the world

stories.title: Iran's ambassador to Russia says turning Iran into another Gaza is impossible
stories.description: Kazem Jalali added that Iran needs victory, either through war or through negotiations, but emphasized that any dialogue must proceed under new conditions

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
